pricing.title: Pay if you want.
pricing.subtitle: Miru will always be free for the essentials. The Sympathisant plan exists for those who want to support the project — not to unlock features.
pricing.freeDescription: Everything essential. No ads, no limits.
pricing.proDescription: Support development without changing your experience.
pricing.noLockBody: The Sympathisant plan grants nothing the free version doesn't — it's a contribution, not a paywall. If in doubt, stick with free, all good.
pricing.alreadyActive: You've been a Sympathisant since {date} — thank you.
pricing.manageInSettings: You can manage your subscription from settings.

settings.quietSubtitle: No notifications between these two hours.
settings.dangerDescription: Deleting your account erases watchlist, reviews and history. Irreversible.

tfa.backupSubtitle1: Save these recovery codes somewhere safe. They let you sign in if you lose your phone.

aboutPage.metaDescription: Miru — an anime platform to explore, organize and share. Open source, ad-free, powered by AniList and MyAnimeList.
aboutPage.heroDescription: Miru is an anime platform built to disappear behind the works. No decorative flourishes, no ads, no tracking — just a clean catalog, an editorial page per anime, and a personal watchlist.
aboutPage.pillar1Body: A catalog built on AniList and enriched via Jikan. Search, filters, sort by community rating — no infinite scroll, no autoplay.
aboutPage.pillar2Body: Personal watchlist with 5 statuses, episode-by-episode progress, and a private rating. Your data stays yours.
aboutPage.pillar3Body: Publish public reviews, read others', follow your history. Each anime's community rating is computed from Miru reviews — distinct from the imported AniList rating.
aboutPage.sourceAccentValue: Computed server-side via sharp (cover hue extraction)
aboutPage.openSourceBody: Issues, PRs and suggestions welcome. Miru is a personal project growing in public — every release is documented.

termsPage.metaDescription: Miru terms of use and privacy policy.
termsPage.title: Terms of use
termsPage.termsBody: Miru is a personal, free service. By using it, you agree not to overload it (reasonable rate-limit), not to use it to scrape the catalog, and not to publish illegal content, harassment or spam. Your data stays yours: you can export it any time, and deleting your account wipes everything within 30 days.
termsPage.privacyBody: We collect the bare minimum: email (for login), handle, and the actions you choose to publish (reviews, public watchlist). No third-party pixel, no third-party analytics, no data resale. Server logs are anonymized after 7 days.
termsPage.cookiesBody: A single session cookie (httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax) to keep you signed in. No marketing or analytics cookies.
termsPage.licensesBody: Catalog data provided by AniList (CC BY-SA 4.0) and MyAnimeList via Jikan (with respect for their rate limits). Fonts Clash Display & General Sans (Fontshare, OFL), JetBrains Mono (OFL). Miru code under MIT license.

changelogPage.title: Changelog
changelogPage.v050Item1: New /genre/[slug] page with stats and curated description
changelogPage.v042Item1: AniList circuit breaker — end of log spam during outages
changelogPage.v042Item2: Hentai filter (3 layers: GraphQL, adapter, repo)
changelogPage.v042Item3: Sticky anime detail header with scroll swap
changelogPage.v040Item3: Reviews with 1-10 slider and spoiler toggle
changelogPage.v040Item4: Watchlist with episode stepper and mini rating slider

genrePage.emptyGenre: No anime indexed for this genre.
genrePage.defaultDescription: All the titles in this genre from our catalog, sorted by community rating.
genrePage.action: Fights, chases, choreographed urgency. Action drives the tempo — sword duels, races, supernatural school battles.
genrePage.adventure: Long journeys, vast worlds, teams that form along the way. Adventure takes its time and rewards patience.
genrePage.comedy: From gag to otaku sitcom — comedy spans every sub-genre, from cozy slice of life to unbridled parody.
genrePage.drama: Introspective stories, intimate conflicts and big emotions. Slice of life, coming-of-age, social drama all rolled in.
genrePage.ecchi: Suggestive without crossing the line. Owned-up fanservice, spicy romantic comedies, light harems.
genrePage.fantasy: Magic, parallel worlds, mythology. Classic heroic fantasy, contemporary isekai, and anything that opens a portal.
genrePage.horror: Tension, the supernatural, oppressive atmosphere. From haunted house to cosmic dread to body horror.
genrePage.mahou-shoujo: Magical girls, transformations, friendships that save the world. The genre defying conventions since Sailor Moon.
genrePage.mecha: Giant robots, teenage pilots, political conflicts in the background. Real robot or super robot, mecha frames a generation.
genrePage.music: Idols, bands, conservatories. When music is the narrative engine, not just the soundtrack.
genrePage.mystery: Puzzles, investigations, late reveals. From classic whodunit to psychological thriller.
genrePage.psychological: Manipulation, moral ambiguity, minds breaking. When the plot plays out inside the characters' heads.
genrePage.romance: Budding feelings, love triangles, confessions that never come. Tender or painful, sometimes both.
genrePage.sci-fi: Spaceships, AI, near or distant futures. Anime sci-fi, from space opera to cyberpunk to post-apo.
genrePage.slice-of-life: Everyday life with no major stakes. Small moments, calm friendships, patient observation.
genrePage.sports: Tournaments, sweat, teams finding themselves. Volleyball, basketball, track — the mechanics of self-transcendence.
genrePage.supernatural: Spirits, demons, powers hidden behind routine. The supernatural drops into far-too-normal high schools.
genrePage.thriller: Constant tension, manhunts, races against the clock. More frantic than mystery, more grounded than horror.
